briefdescription.para: create a U1 symmetry object

detaileddescription.para: create a new U1 symmetry object that serves as a generator. The symmetry object is a property of Bond . It is used to identify the symmetry of the quantum number set, as well as providing the combining rule for the quantum number when Bonds are combined.

briefdescription.para: create a Zn discrete symmetry object with $n\in\mathbb{N}$

detaileddescription.para: create a new Zn discrete symmetry object with integer $ n $ that serves as a generator. The symmetry object is a property of Bond . It is used to identify the symmetry of the quantum number set, as well as providing the combining rule for the quantum number when Bonds are combined.

briefdescription.para: create a fermionic parity symmetry object

detaileddescription.para: \f$[0 , 1]\f$ where 0 corresponds to even parity and 1 to odd parity;

create a new fermionic parity symmetry object that serves as a generator. The symmetry object is a property of Bond . It is used to identify the symmetry of the quantum number set, as well as providing the combining rule for the quantum number when Bonds are combined.

briefdescription.para: create a fermionic occupation number symmetry object

detaileddescription.para: create a new fermionic occupation number object that serves as a generator. This is a U1 symmetry with parity defined as EVEN for even occupation numbers and ODD for odd occupation numbers. The symmetry object is a property of Bond . It is used to identify the symmetry of the quantum number set, as well as providing the combining rule for the quantum number when Bonds are combined.

briefdescription.para: Load a Symmetry object from a file.
Same as static Symmetry Load(const std::string &fname);.
return a clone instance of current Symmetry object.
return the symmetry type-id of current Symmetry object, see cytnx::SymmetryType::
return the discrete n of current Symmetry object.

listitem.para: for U1, n=1 will be returned.
for Zn, n is the discrete symmetry number. (ex: Z2, n=2)

briefdescription.para: return the symmetry type name of current Symmetry object in string form, see cytnx::SymmetryType::
check the quantum number qnum is within the valid value range of current Symmetry.
check all the quantum numbers \qnums are within the valid value range of current Symmetry.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< cytnx_int64 > &inL, const std::vector< cytnx_int64 > &inR) const
memberdef.name: combine_rule

briefdescription.para: apply combine rule of current symmetry to two quantum number lists.

parameterdescription.para: the #1 quantum number list that is to be combined.
the #2 quantum number list that is to be combined.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::vector< cytnx_int64 > &out, const std::vector< cytnx_int64 > &inL, const std::vector< cytnx_int64 > &inR) const
memberdef.name: combine_rule_

briefdescription.para: apply combine rule of current symmetry to two quantum number lists, and store it into parameter out.

parameterdescription.para: the #1 quantum number list that is to be combined.
the #2 quantum number list that is to be combined.

memberdef.argsstring: (const cytnx_int64 &inL, const cytnx_int64 &inR, const bool &is_reverse=false) const
memberdef.name: combine_rule

briefdescription.para: apply combine rule of current symmetry to two quantum numbers.

memberdef.argsstring: (cytnx_int64 &out, const cytnx_int64 &inL, const cytnx_int64 &inR, const bool &is_reverse=false) const
memberdef.name: combine_rule_

briefdescription.para: apply combine rule of current symmetry to two quantum numbers, and store the combined quntun number into parameter
Apply reverse rule of current symmetry to a given quantum number and store in parameter out.

detaileddescription.para:
that means, $ o = -i $, where $ o $ is the output quantum number out, and $ i $ is the input quantum number in.
out
the output quantum number.
in
the input quantum number.

briefdescription.para: Apply reverse rule of current symmetry to a given quantum number and return the result.

detaileddescription.para:
that means, $ o = -i $, where $ o $ is the reverse quantum number, and $ i $ is the input quantum number in.
in
the input quantum number.
the reverse quantum number.

briefdescription.para: fermionic parity for a given quantum number

simplesect.para: parity of the quantum number; false for bosonic degree of freedom, true for fermionic

briefdescription.para: check if the Symmetry is fermionic or not
Save the current Symmetry object to a file.

simplesect.para: the file extension will be automatically added as ".cysym".

briefdescription.para: Same as Save(const std::string &fname) const;.
Print the information of current Symmetry object.
the equality operator of the Symmetry object.
the inequality operator of the Symmetry object.
the symmetry object
